2605.17873.title: HINT-SD: Targeted Hindsight Self-Distillation for Long-Horizon Agents
2605.17873.paper_summary: Training long-horizon LLM agents with reinforcement learning is challenging because sparse outcome rewards reveal whether a task succeeds, but not which intermediate actions caused the outcome or how they should be corrected. Recent methods alleviate this issue by generating rewards or textual hints from turn-level action-output signals, or by using feedback-conditioned self-distillation. However, generating feedback at every turn is inefficient when many intermediate turns are already successful or neutral, and applying feedback at a fixed or misaligned turn often fails to supervise the actions that contributed to the failure. To bridge this gap, we propose HINT-SD, a targeted self-distillation framework that uses full-trajectory hindsight to select failure-relevant actions and applies feedback-conditioned distillation only on targeted action spans. Experiments on BFCL v3 and AppWorld show that our method improves over the dense per-turn feedback baseline by up to 18.80 percent while achieving 2.26$\times$ lower time per training step, suggesting that selecting where to distill is a key factor for both effective and efficient long-horizon agent training.
2605.17873.paper_summary_zh: 訓練長期 LLM 代理人使用強化學習是具有挑戰性的，因為稀疏的結果獎勵只能顯示任務是否成功，但無法顯示哪些中間行動導致了結果或應該如何修正。最近的方法通過從回合級行動輸出信號生成獎勵或文本提示，或使用反饋條件自我蒸餾來緩解這一問題。然而，當許多中間回合已經成功或中立時，在每個回合生成反饋是低效的，而在固定或不對齊的回合施加反饋往往無法監督導致失敗的行動。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了 HINT-SD，一個有針對性的自我蒸餾框架，使用全軌跡的事後回顧來選擇與失敗相關的行動，並僅在目標行動範圍內應用反饋條件的蒸餾。在 BFCL v3 和 AppWorld 上的實驗顯示，我們的方法在密集的每回合反饋基準上提高了多達 18.80% 的表現，同時每個訓練步驟的時間降低了 2.26$\times$，這表明選擇蒸餾的地點是有效且高效的長期代理人訓練的關鍵因素。
2605.17873.author: Woongyeng Yeo et.al.
2605.17873.authors: Woongyeng Yeo, Yumin Choi, Taekyung Ki, Sung Ju Hwang
